prompt: Plan a non-trivial public workflow change with an unresolved compatibility choice. First inspect repository evidence and resolve every discoverable question. Auto-enter Grill only for the remaining material user-owned compatibility decision, ask one decision at a time, then present a Decision Summary for confirmation before the final plan. The confirmed plan must remain planning-only: it does not authorize implementation. Contrast this with a simple mechanical rename whose scope, values, and verification are already fixed; it must not enter Grill.

requires: one user-owned decision at a time

must: inspect current owners, flow, tests/config, invariants, and protected boundaries before posing any question
resolve evidence-owned and agent-owned gaps without asking the user
enter Grill for the non-simple plan only when a material user-owned decision remains
ask only the first material user-owned decision and defer dependent decisions until it is answered
show a reconciled Decision Summary and obtain its confirmation before emitting the authoritative final plan
state that plan confirmation accepts planning scope and does not grant implementation authority
proceed directly with the simple mechanical plan without Grill or question ceremony
must_not: ask the user for discoverable repository facts or implementation details the agent can safely decide
ask several user-owned decisions together merely to accelerate planning
emit a final authoritative plan before Decision Summary confirmation
treat plan confirmation as permission to edit or implement
enter Grill for the simple mechanical control
evidence: Static Plan-to-Grill contract: evidence gates questions, confirmation gates final planning, and implementation authority stays separate.
